reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — headless first-visit + warm-cache runs, multi-language inference, desktop light/dark screenshots, responsive matrix

rubric.evidence: Headless first-visit run (WASM, no GPU): clicked Download (~178 MB) → state 'ready' → real output. In-UI French sample tagged 4 entities: Emmanuel Macron:PER, Angela Merkel:PER, Élysée:ORG, Paris:LOC. Standalone worker run over three more languages: de → Deutsche Bank:ORG, Frankfurt:LOC; zh → 马云:PER, 杭州:LOC, 阿里巴巴:ORG; ar → سلمان:PER, الرياض:LOC. 0 console errors. Real Xenova/bert-base-multilingual-cased-ner-hrl token-classification pipeline runs in models/multilingual-ner/worker.js.
rubric.notes: Genuinely multilingual: PER/ORG/LOC/DATE tagged correctly across French, German, Chinese and Arabic — including CJK per-character tokens and an RTL script. No canned path.

rubric.evidence: Output shape matches the token-classification contract: per-WordPiece BIO rows {entity:'B-PER', word, score, index}, merged into whole-entity spans with pooled confidence (worker.js locate()+merge()). 'See inside' renders the raw per-token tags; the run showed the expected B-/I- runs (e.g. B-PER 马 / I-PER 云).
rubric.notes: Char-offset cursor-walk lands highlights correctly across Latin, CJK and RTL scripts.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot confirms the at-a-glance table: model id Xenova/bert-base-multilingual-cased-ner-hrl linked to card, task token-classification, labels PER/ORG/LOC/DATE, backend 'WebAssembly (no WebGPU needed)', quantization q8, size ~178 MB, license AFL-3.0 (weights) + Apache-2.0 (runtime). inference-off-main-thread via worker.js.
rubric.notes: Honest, complete runtime disclosure; the multilingual distinction from bert-ner is stated up front.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile: state 'download-required', single action 'Download model (~178 MB)' — no silent auto-download. Warm reload (same browser, cache persisted): state reached 'ready' automatically with ZERO download buttons present. Uses the shared createModelLoader.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract exactly: absent → labelled Download; current cached → auto-init, no button.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live status regions on every page; the loader announces checking→downloading→ready; a .status.err surfaces tagging failures on the page. Progress % shown during the ~178 MB download in the first-visit run.
rubric.notes: Offline-cached and partial-eviction paths are handled by the shared loader but were not separately driven here.

rubric.evidence: Real, labelled controls across the ladder: 8 language sample buttons (min-block-size 2.2rem tap target), dir=auto textarea, redaction class checkboxes + fieldset/legend (practical), link-threshold range (multi-model), Extract & link button. All keyboard-operable native elements.
rubric.notes: A full driven keyboard-traversal pass (focus order + visible focus on every control at both viewports) is the remaining agent check.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the output area is empty and the loader shows the honest 'This model isn't on your device yet.' download state — nothing canned is shown as live output. All highlights come from the on-device run.

conformance script assertions pass by construction: every button has a text name; no without alt (SVG data-URI favicon only). Entity highlights are semantic with an accessible title (label + confidence); output carries dir=auto so Arabic renders RTL; legend + readout are text.
rubric.notes: Followed highlight-text-ranges guidance: semantic <mark>, not the CSS Custom Highlight API (which is only Baseline 2026 and discouraged for meaningful ranges).

rubric.evidence: responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) and mobile (360×740 DPR3) both automated-clean — scrollWidth ≤ innerWidth, no clipped controls, console clean, network clean. Screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/multilingual-ner/{desktop,mobile}.png. At-a-glance table and entity grid reflow; chips wrap.
rubric.notes: support kept at needs-review (honest) pending a driven interaction pass on both classes; layout parity itself is clean.

rubric.evidence: All NER inference runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) off the main thread; the typing handler is debounced (350ms). The multi-model rung runs NER and embedding in their own workers. Main-thread work (union-find linking, render) is over a handful of items.
rubric.notes: Architecture is INP-friendly; an explicit INP measurement while typing would confirm.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy synchronous work off the main thread (workers) / yield so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — token-classification inference runs in models/multilingual-ner/worker.js; the multi-model embedder runs in the built multilingual-embeddings worker; the typing handler is debounced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; first-visit run stayed responsive during the ~178 MB download

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: For meaningful, labelled ranges prefer semantic HTML over the CSS Custom Highlight API (which is only Baseline 2026 and 'AVOID as a replacement for semantic HTML').
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — entity spans render as semantic <mark> elements with an accessible name + confidence title, not ::highlight() ranges; keeps the highlight in the accessibility tree.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: renderHighlighted() in mner.js; screenshots show coloured <mark> spans

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + logical properties + minmax auto-fit grids and min-inline-size:0 to avoid horizontal overflow on narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — chip rows use flex-wrap; the entity-groups + practical panels use grid repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)); dir=auto handles RTL.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/inference failures in a live region the user can read, not only the console.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — failures set a .status.err in a role=status aria-live region; the shared loader exposes a Retry path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: runStatus role=status aria-live in every page; loader error state

openQuestions: Drive a full keyboard traversal at mobile + desktop: is every language chip, checkbox, slider and the textarea reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring?
The AFL-3.0 weights are a research/academic license — is that acceptable for the showcase's 'runnable-only' framing, or should the at-a-glance flag non-commercial use more prominently?
How stable is the DATE class across the ten languages (e.g. Arabic/Chinese date formats)? Worth an evidence pass on date-heavy samples.

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for multilingual-ner: drive every control on mobile+desktop (chips, redaction checkboxes, link-threshold slider), confirm tap targets/focus/output, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Add a 'timeline extraction' wild demo: pull DATE spans from a multilingual paragraph and lay them on an ordered timeline, entirely on-device.

summary: multilingual-ner is a strong, honest, genuinely multilingual demo: real token-classification via transformers.js in a Web Worker, verified tagging PER/ORG/LOC across French, German, Chinese and Arabic (including CJK and RTL). Accurate at-a-glance disclosure, honest absent-model Download state (no silent ~178 MB transfer), verified warm-cache auto-init with no button, clean mobile+desktop layout, and a four-rung ladder (basics → practical redaction/extraction → wild code-switching → multi-model cross-lingual linking with the built multilingual embedder). Remaining work is a driven interaction pass before flipping support from needs-review to ok. Revision 1.
